status_notes: Initial research from Azure Functions documentation, 2026-04-04. Needs cross-reference pass and human review with live Azure environment.
notes: Serverless compute for Azure. Security focus: authentication, managed identity, network isolation, key management.

urls.note: App Service REST API (Functions share this API)

match_nodes.description: Built-in authentication (Easy Auth) for function apps

data.description: Azure Functions built-in authentication (Easy Auth) provides turn-key authentication and authorization. Runs as middleware before function code executes. Supports multiple identity providers. No code changes required in the function.

options.name: Microsoft Entra ID
options.description: Recommended for enterprise. Supports single-tenant, multi-tenant, and B2C.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Entra ID for production workloads. Enable authentication at the platform level rather than implementing it in function code. Require authentication for all endpoints unless explicitly public.

configure.cli: az webapp auth update --name {app} --resource-group {rg} --enabled true --action LoginWithAzureActiveDirectory
configure.console: Azure Portal → Function App → Authentication → Add identity provider

match_nodes.description: Microsoft Entra ID identity for Functions — eliminates credential management

data.description: Managed identity provides an automatically managed identity in Microsoft Entra ID for the function app. Functions can use this identity to authenticate to Azure services (Key Vault, Storage, SQL, etc.) without storing credentials in code or configuration.

options.name: System-assigned
options.description: Identity tied to the function app lifecycle. Created and deleted with the app.

options.name: User-assigned
options.description: Standalone identity resource. Can be shared across multiple function apps.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use managed identity for all Azure service connections. Eliminate connection strings with embedded credentials from app settings.

match_nodes.description: Run Functions in VNet for private resource access

data.description: VNet integration allows function apps to access resources in an Azure virtual network. Outbound traffic from the function can route through the VNet to reach private endpoints, on-premises resources (via VPN/ExpressRoute), and service endpoints.

options.name: Regional VNet integration
options.description: Connects to a subnet in the same region. Premium or Dedicated (App Service) plan required.

options.name: Gateway-required VNet integration
options.description: Legacy. Connects via VPN gateway. Supports cross-region. Being superseded by regional.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use regional VNet integration with Premium plan for production workloads that access private resources. Route all outbound traffic through VNet for network-level controls.

data.description: Azure Functions uses authorization keys to restrict access to HTTP-triggered functions. Three levels: function keys (per-function), host keys (all functions in the app), and the master key (admin access including runtime APIs). Keys are passed as query parameter or x-functions-key header.

options.name: Function key
options.description: Scoped to a single function. Multiple keys per function allowed.

options.name: Host key
options.description: Grants access to all functions in the app. Named keys for different consumers.

options.name: Master key
options.description: Admin-level access. Includes access to runtime management APIs. Cannot be revoked, only renewed.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use Entra ID authentication instead of keys for production. If keys are required: rotate regularly, use function-level keys (not host/master), store keys in Key Vault, never embed in client code.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Function App → App keys

configure.note: Authorization level is set in function.json or function attribute: anonymous, function, admin.

match_nodes.description: Restrict function access to private network

data.description: Private endpoints provide a private IP address within a VNet for the function app. Inbound traffic reaches the function through the private endpoint instead of the public internet. Used to completely eliminate public access to the function.

options.name: Private endpoint
options.description: Private IP in VNet. Premium or Dedicated plan required.

options.name: Access restrictions
options.description: IP-based or service tag-based restrictions on public endpoint. Available on all plans.

data.vendor_recommendation: Use private endpoints for functions that should not be accessible from the public internet. Combine with VNet integration for fully private networking (inbound and outbound).

match_nodes.description: Application settings encrypted at rest with Key Vault references

data.description: Function app settings (environment variables) are encrypted at rest by Azure. For secrets, Key Vault references allow app settings to point to Key Vault secrets instead of containing the secret value directly. The function app uses managed identity to access Key Vault at runtime.
data.vendor_recommendation: Use Key Vault references for all secrets (connection strings, API keys, certificates). Never store raw secrets in app settings. Use managed identity for Key Vault access.

audit.note: Key Vault references appear as @Microsoft.KeyVault(SecretUri={uri}) or @Microsoft.KeyVault(VaultName={vault};SecretName={name}).

configure.terraform: app_settings with Key Vault reference syntax on azurerm_linux_function_app
